name: Maharashtra
stopoverNote: Mumbai's Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport is Maharashtra's natural international gateway; spend 2 days in South Mumbai before continuing inland to Aurangabad, Nashik and Pune.

diet.vegetarian: Maharashtra is highly vegetarian-friendly. Misal pav, poha, sabudana khichdi, vada pav and Maharashtrian thalis are easy to find; local thalis usually run about ₹120–₹300 at simple restaurants.
diet.vegan: Ask for 'ghee nakko, butter nakko' and avoid shrikhand, curd, buttered pav and ghee-heavy puran poli. Solkadhi, poha, many chaats and oil-cooked bhakri-sabzi meals are often vegan when ordered without dairy.
phrases: Mee shaakahari aahe = I am vegetarian
Doodh, dahi, toop, loni nako = no milk, curd, ghee or butter
Ghee nakko, butter nakko = no ghee, no butter

cities.name: Mumbai
cities.notes: India's financial capital and Bollywood heartland — Art Deco boulevards, colonial UNESCO heritage, museums, street food and Arabian Sea promenades.

cities.name: Aurangabad
cities.notes: Gateway to Ajanta and Ellora, plus Bibi Ka Maqbara, Daulatabad Fort and Mughal-era Deccan heritage.

cities.name: Nashik
cities.notes: India's wine capital around Sula Vineyards, with sacred Godavari ghats, Trimbakeshwar Jyotirlinga and Pandavleni Buddhist caves.

cities.name: Pune
cities.notes: Maharashtra's cultural capital — Peshwa heritage, Gandhi history, cafés, street food and hill-fort excursions before the Mumbai return.

itinerary.sim: Any Indian prepaid SIM from Jio or Airtel works well across Mumbai, Pune, Nashik and Aurangabad; carry offline maps for Ajanta/Ellora day trips where hill-road coverage can drop.

Mumbai.name: Mumbai
Mumbai.note: Two days balances South Mumbai's heritage core with the half-day Elephanta ferry. Monsoon months can disrupt ferries, trains and seafront plans.

days.note: Gateway of India, CST and CSMVS sit in the South Mumbai heritage belt; start early, then wander Colaba before traffic builds.

activities.name: Gateway of India at sunrise
activities.tip: Best from 7–9am before tour buses and ferry crowds arrive.

activities.name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus — UNESCO Gothic station
activities.cost: Free to enter station; Heritage Gallery tour ₹200
activities.tip: View the façade from across the road; book the gallery if you want interior architecture context.

activities.name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Vastu Sangrahalaya museum
activities.tip: Closed Mondays; the Indo-Saracenic building and sculpture galleries are the highlights.

activities.name: Colaba Causeway Market & Leopold Café
activities.tip: Bargain gently for textiles, silver jewelry and vintage Bollywood posters.

days.theme: Elephanta caves, temples & the sea promenade
days.note: Elephanta is a half-day ferry trip from Gateway of India and is closed Mondays; ferries typically run from morning until mid-afternoon, with last returns around evening.

activities.name: Elephanta Caves — UNESCO Shiva rock-cut temples
activities.cost: Ferry ₹150–₹200 round-trip; entry ₹40 Indians / ₹600 foreigners; toy train ₹10
activities.tip: Take the earliest practical ferry, carry water, and pre-book ASI tickets if queues are heavy.

activities.name: Siddhivinayak Temple
activities.tip: Avoid Tuesdays and major festivals if you dislike long queues.

activities.name: Marine Drive and Chowpatty evening stroll
activities.tip: Arrive at twilight for the Queen's Necklace lights.

activities.name: Bandra Bandstand or Dharavi guided walk
activities.tip: Choose Bandra for sea views and street art, Dharavi only with an ethical guided operator.

Aurangabad.name: Aurangabad
Aurangabad.note: Plan three separate days: Aurangabad city, Ajanta, then Ellora. Ajanta and Ellora cannot be combined honestly — they are in opposite directions from Aurangabad, each needs 4–5 hours on-site, Ajanta closes Mondays and Ellora closes Tuesdays.

days.theme: Aurangabad city — Mughal tombs and medieval fort
days.note: Keep city sights separate from the cave excursions; Bibi Ka Maqbara, Daulatabad and Panchakki make a full but realistic local day.

activities.name: Bibi Ka Maqbara — the 'Mini Taj Mahal'
activities.tip: Go in soft morning or golden-hour light; timings are usually 8am–8pm.

activities.name: Daulatabad Fort
activities.tip: Bring a torch for the dark passage and expect a steep final climb.

activities.name: Panchakki Water Mill & dargah complex
activities.tip: A calm Sufi complex and good low-effort stop after the fort climb.

activities.name: Aurangabad Caves
activities.tip: A quiet Buddhist cave teaser if you still have afternoon energy.

days.theme: Ajanta Caves — Buddhist murals and horseshoe gorge
days.note: Ajanta is a full-day trip 105km from Aurangabad and is closed Mondays. Do not pair it with Ellora; the travel directions and on-site time make that unrealistic.

activities.name: Ajanta Caves — 30 Buddhist rock-cut caves
activities.cost: ₹40 Indians / ₹600 foreigners; MAHABUS shuttle ₹15–₹20; parking about ₹75
activities.tip: Prioritize Caves 1, 2, 16, 17 and 26; no flash photography because it damages ancient pigments.

activities.name: Licensed guide for Ajanta narrative art
activities.tip: A guide makes the Jataka murals and Buddhist iconography far easier to understand.

activities.name: Ajanta horseshoe-gorge viewpoint
activities.tip: Do the overlook before fatigue sets in; it frames the whole cliff arc.

activities.name: Aurangabad–Ajanta round-trip transport
activities.cost: Taxi ₹2,500–₹3,600 round-trip; MSRTC bus about ₹100 each way
activities.tip: Leave by 8am and confirm the return pickup time before entering the site.

days.note: Ellora is 30km from Aurangabad and closed Tuesdays. It is much closer than Ajanta, but still deserves its own day because Kailasa alone can absorb hours.

activities.name: Ellora Caves — Buddhist, Hindu and Jain complex
activities.tip: Start at Kailasa Temple, Cave 16 — the monolithic scale is the whole point of Ellora.

activities.name: Kailasa Temple detailed circuit
activities.tip: Walk the upper and lower levels to understand the top-down carving.

activities.name: Grishneshwar Jyotirlinga Temple
activities.tip: Active temple beside Ellora; dress modestly and avoid intrusive photography.

activities.name: Ellora guide or audio context
activities.tip: Helpful for distinguishing Buddhist, Hindu and Jain phases across the 34 caves.

Nashik.name: Nashik
Nashik.note: One focused day covers a dawn temple visit, Buddhist caves, Godavari ghats and a late-afternoon vineyard session; add a second only for more wineries or Brahmagiri hiking.

days.theme: Sacred Godavari, Buddhist caves & Sula sunset
days.note: Start at Trimbakeshwar before crowds, return through Nashik city sights, then save Sula for the softer 4–7pm vineyard light.

activities.name: Trimbakeshwar Jyotirlinga Temple
activities.tip: Visit at dawn; men may need traditional dress near the sanctum.

activities.name: Pandavleni / Trirashmi Buddhist Caves
activities.tip: Climb the steps before midday heat; verify local closure reports before going.

activities.name: Panchavati and Ramkund Ghats
activities.tip: Combine Ramkund with nearby Kalaram Temple for a compact sacred-river circuit.

activities.name: Sula Vineyards tour and tasting
activities.cost: Entry ₹600 weekdays / ₹1,000 weekends; Best of Sula tasting ₹600 weekdays / ₹700 weekends; personalized tasting ₹1,500 pp
activities.tip: Book the tasting in advance for Oct–Feb weekends; entry is usually redeemable against wine or food.

Pune.name: Pune
Pune.note: Two days gives one city day for Peshwa and Gandhi sites, then one fort-and-viewpoint day before returning to Mumbai via the expressway or train.

days.theme: Peshwa heritage, Gandhi history & Pune cafés
days.note: Old-city heritage sights cluster tightly, while Aga Khan Palace and Koregaon Park need short auto rides across town.

activities.name: Shaniwar Wada — Peshwa palace fortifications
activities.tip: Go early for the gates and ramparts; show timings vary seasonally.

activities.name: Pataleshwar Cave Temple
activities.tip: A compact 8th-century rock-cut Shiva temple hidden in central Pune.

activities.name: Aga Khan Palace — Gandhi Memorial Museum
activities.tip: The gardens and Kasturba Gandhi memorial are as important as the museum rooms.

activities.name: FC Road cafés, Joshi's Vadapav and Kayani Bakery
activities.cost: Vada pav ₹20–₹40; café meal ₹250–₹600; Shrewsbury biscuits about ₹300/tin
activities.tip: Kayani sells out; go earlier in the day if biscuits matter.

activities.name: Osho International Meditation Resort area
activities.cost: Day-pass access varies, about ₹600–₹1,200; verify at osho.com
activities.tip: Even without a session, Koregaon Park is a pleasant leafy café walk.

days.theme: Sinhagad Fort, Parvati Hill & Mumbai return prep
days.note: Sinhagad is a half-day hill-fort outing 30km southwest. Start early, then keep the afternoon flexible for Parvati Hill or onward Mumbai travel.

activities.name: Sinhagad Fort morning hike or jeep ride
activities.cost: Entry about ₹35; shared jeep ₹100–₹150 from base
activities.tip: Weekends are crowded; arrive by 7am and try pitla bhakri at the top.

activities.name: Parvati Hill and temple complex
activities.tip: A gentle 103-step climb with a broad city panorama.

activities.name: Pune–Mumbai expressway or Deccan Queen planning
activities.tip: Book a late-afternoon bus/train if flying out of Mumbai the next day.

connections.method: Train (Tapovan/Devagiri) or 1h flight; overnight AC bus if needed
Train, MSRTC/private bus or hired taxi via Yeola
MSRTC/private AC bus, train or hired taxi
MSRTC Shivneri/Volvo bus, Deccan Queen/Shatabdi train or short flight

extras: Ajanta and Ellora are not same-day sights: Ajanta is 105km from Aurangabad and closed Mondays, while Ellora is 30km away in the opposite direction and closed Tuesdays. Keep them on separate days.
Pre-book ASI tickets for Elephanta, Ajanta and Ellora through the ASI portal when travelling in peak Oct–Mar season.

June–September is monsoon: Mumbai flooding can disrupt local trains and ferries, while cave roads may be slippery. Western Ghats waterfalls are beautiful, but this heritage circuit is smoother in winter.
Nashik's Sula Vineyards is busiest on weekends; the Best of Sula tour is ₹600 weekdays / ₹700 weekends, while entry is ₹600 weekdays / ₹1,000 weekends and typically redeemable.
Goa is the easiest beach add-on after Pune or Mumbai by overnight train/bus; Gujarat and Karnataka are better 3–5 day extensions for heritage and wildlife.
